name: Green Chili with White Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 cups green onions and their tops sliced
6 cans diced green chiles (7 oz each)
3 pounds pork shoulder trimmed of fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly slice the bell peppers across.
2. Heat oil in a 10-quart pot over medium-high heat; add the bell peppers, onions, garlic, and cumin. Cook, stirring until onions are soft.
3. Add tomatillos (breaking them up with a spoon) and their liquid, chiles, beans, pork, oregano, and red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer until pork is tender when pierced (about 2 hours).
- For a thin chili, cook covered.
- For a thicker chili, cook uncovered to desired consistency. Stir occasionally.
4. Remove from heat. Cover and refrigerate for up to 3 days. Reheat before continuing.
5. Reserve a few cilantro leaves; chop the remaining leaves. Stir chopped cilantro into the chili; garnish with reserved leaves. Serve.
